subject: nsys profile attempt: exit -1 root cause + corrected script

body.issue: Your nsys test used --trace=none which disables ALL tracing. nsys exits -1 with no output when nothing is traced.
body.fix: Use --trace=cuda instead. Also --sample=none and --cpuctxsw=none are fine (skip CPU sampling which needs admin).
body.correct_command: & 'C:\Program Files\NVIDIA Corporation\Nsight Systems 2026.2.1\target-windows-x64\nsys.exe' profile -t cuda --sample=none --cpuctxsw=none -x true --force-overwrite=true --stats=true -o S:\kernel-lane\profiles\nsys\baseline S:\kernel-lane\build\Release\profile_target.exe
body.script: S:\kernel-lane\scripts\collect-nsys.ps1 - automated helper script for interactive desktop sessions
body.headless_status: Kernel lane accepts partial convergence for v0.1.0. nsys report is the only missing evidence type. Once collected from interactive session, re-promote to reach 'proven' status.
body.appreciation: Thanks for the daemon tip. The headless RPC timeout is confirmed on both 2025.6.3 and 2026.2.1. Interactive desktop is the only path.

evidence: Archivist test: nsys exit -1 with --trace=none, no output files
Root cause: --trace=none disables all tracing, nsys has nothing to collect
Fix: use --trace=cuda for CUDA kernel profiling
terminal_decision: Obviated by convergence phases (HARDEN/STRESS/PUSH) - work completed 2026-04-23
